---
title: >
  <table style="width:100%; border:none; border-collapse:collapse; background:transparent; cellpadding:0; cellspacing:0;">
  <tr>
    <td style="border:none; padding:0; vertical-align:middle; text-align:left;">
      <div style="font-size:1.25em; font-weight:bold; line-height:1.1;">Multilevel Modeling</div>
      <div style="font-size:1.25em; font-weight:bold; line-height:1.1;">in Clinical Research</div>
    </td>
    <td style="border:none; padding:0; vertical-align:middle; text-align:right;">
      <img src="`r here::here('slides','img','cfa_icon_emoji.png')`" style="width:150px; display:inline-block; margin-left:-10px;">
    </td>
  </tr> </table>
subtitle: "<small>Fitting and Interpreting</small>"
author: "`r paste(params$author, collapse = ' & ')`"
date: "`r paste0(params$location, ', ', params$date)`"
params:
  author: ["Author Name"]
  date: "Placeholder Date"
  location: "Placeholder Location"
---
exclude: true

```{r child = "_setup.Rmd"}
```

```{r title_emoji, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE}

library(DiagrammeR)
library(DiagrammeRsvg)
library(rsvg)
library(here)

# Create the graph with smaller arrows and transparent background
graph <- grViz("
digraph cfa_icon_emoji {

  graph [
    layout=dot,
    rankdir=TB,
    bgcolor=transparent,
    ranksep=0.25,
    nodesep=0.15
  ];

  // Nodes with thick black borders
  node [
    shape=circle,
    width=0.60,
    height=0.60,
    fixedsize=true,
    label='',
    style=filled,
    color='black',     // border color
    penwidth=2.5         // border thickness
  ];

  school [fillcolor='#FF8A80'];
  classroom1 [fillcolor='#FFB199'];
  classroom2 [fillcolor='#FFB199'];

  student1 [fillcolor='#FFD8C2'];
  student2 [fillcolor='#FFD8C2'];
  student3 [fillcolor='#FFD8C2'];
  student4 [fillcolor='#FFD8C2'];

  // Black arrows
  edge [
    arrowsize=0.6,
    penwidth=2,
    color='black',
    headclip=true,
    tailclip=true
  ];

  school -> classroom1;
  school -> classroom2;
  classroom1 -> student1;
  classroom1 -> student2;
  classroom2 -> student3;
  classroom2 -> student4;

  { rank=same; classroom1; classroom2; }
}
")


# Export to SVG
svg_graph <- export_svg(graph)

# Save PNG, transparent by default
rsvg_png(
  charToRaw(svg_graph),
  file = here::here("slides", "img", "cfa_icon_emoji.png"),
  width = 1200,
  height = 1200
)

```

```{r models, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=T}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.retina = 3
)

library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(ggplot2)
library(performance)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# ── Reproducible data ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set.seed(42)
n_clinics   <- 25
clinic_sizes <- sample(11:32, n_clinics, replace = TRUE)

clinic_data <- data.frame(
  clinic_id      = 1:n_clinics,
  clinic_size    = sample(80:220, n_clinics, replace = TRUE),
  protocol_type  = rbinom(n_clinics, 1, 0.5)
)
clinic_data$size50 <- (clinic_data$clinic_size - 150) / 50
clinic_data$u0c    <- rnorm(n_clinics, 0, 3.69)

patient_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:n_clinics, function(c) {
  n <- clinic_sizes[c]
  data.frame(
    clinic_id      = c,
    patient_id     = seq_len(n),
    clinic_size    = clinic_data$clinic_size[c],
    size50         = clinic_data$size50[c],
    protocol_type  = clinic_data$protocol_type[c],
    pain_score     = 49.2 + clinic_data$u0c[c] + rnorm(n, 0, 6.28)
  )
}))

# === Models ======================================================================
Model0 <- lm(pain_score ~ 1, data = patient_data)
Model1 <- lmer(pain_score ~ 1 + (1 | clinic_id), data = patient_data, REML = TRUE)
Model2 <- lmer(pain_score ~ 1 + size50 + protocol_type + (1 | clinic_id),
               data = patient_data, REML = TRUE)
Model3 <- lmer(pain_score ~ 1 + size50 * protocol_type + (1 | clinic_id),
               data = patient_data, REML = TRUE)

# === Variance components =========================================================
vc1      <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(Model1))
tau_u0   <- vc1$vcov[1]
sigma_e  <- vc1$vcov[2]
icc_manual       <- tau_u0 / (tau_u0 + sigma_e)
avg_cluster_size <- mean(table(patient_data$clinic_id))
design_effect    <- 1 + (avg_cluster_size - 1) * icc_manual
effective_n      <- nrow(patient_data) / design_effect
icc2_value       <- tau_u0 / (tau_u0 + sigma_e / avg_cluster_size)

fixed_int <- fixef(Model1)[1]
ri_sd     <- sqrt(tau_u0)
ri_lower  <- fixed_int - 1.96 * ri_sd
ri_upper  <- fixed_int + 1.96 * ri_sd

coef_table2 <- summary(Model2, ddf = "Satterthwaite")$coefficients
coef_table3 <- summary(Model3, ddf = "Satterthwaite")$coefficients

Model1_ML <- lmer(pain_score ~ 1 + (1 | clinic_id),
                  data = patient_data, REML = FALSE)
Model2_ML <- lmer(pain_score ~ 1 + size50 + protocol_type + (1 | clinic_id),
                  data = patient_data, REML = FALSE)
Model3_ML <- lmer(pain_score ~ 1 + size50 * protocol_type + (1 | clinic_id),
                  data = patient_data, REML = FALSE)

lrt_stat     <- -2 * (logLik(Model0) - logLik(Model1))
lrt_compare  <- anova(Model1_ML, Model2_ML)
lrt_compare23 <- anova(Model2_ML, Model3_ML)

vc2       <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(Model2_ML))
cond_icc  <- vc2$vcov[1] / sum(vc2$vcov)

# ── PseudoR2 helper ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PseudoR2 <- function(baseModel, model1, model2 = NULL) {
  vcb  <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(baseModel))
  vc1_ <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(model1))
  r2L2_1 <- (vcb$vcov[1] - vc1_$vcov[1]) / vcb$vcov[1]
  r2L1_1 <- (vcb$vcov[2] - vc1_$vcov[2]) / vcb$vcov[2]
  res <- data.frame(
    Component       = c("Between-Clinic (L2)", "Within-Clinic (L1)"),
    Base_Var        = round(c(vcb$vcov[1],  vcb$vcov[2]),  3),
    Model1_Var      = round(c(vc1_$vcov[1], vc1_$vcov[2]), 3),
    PseudoR2_M1     = round(c(r2L2_1, r2L1_1), 4)
  )
  if (!is.null(model2)) {
    vc2_ <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(model2))
    r2L2_2 <- (vcb$vcov[1] - vc2_$vcov[1]) / vcb$vcov[1]
    r2L1_2 <- (vcb$vcov[2] - vc2_$vcov[2]) / vcb$vcov[2]
    res$Model2_Var  <- round(c(vc2_$vcov[1], vc2_$vcov[2]), 3)
    res$PseudoR2_M2 <- round(c(r2L2_2, r2L1_2), 4)
    res$Change      <- round(res$PseudoR2_M2 - res$PseudoR2_M1, 4)
  }
  res
}

pseudo_r2_all <- PseudoR2(Model1_ML, Model2_ML, Model3_ML)

# ── TotalR2 helper ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
patient_data$pred2 <- predict(Model2, re.form = NA)
patient_data$pred3 <- predict(Model3, re.form = NA)
totalR2_2 <- cor(patient_data$pain_score, patient_data$pred2, use = "complete.obs")^2
totalR2_3 <- cor(patient_data$pain_score, patient_data$pred3, use = "complete.obs")^2

```

---

## `r ji('clipboard')` Study Overview

**The Research Question**

<small>

> **Can clinic-level characteristics predict patient pain?**

</small>

.pull-left-50[

**The Data**

`r ji('hospital')` **`r dplyr::n_distinct(patient_data$clinic_id)`** clinics 

`r ji('face_with_thermometer')` **`r nrow(patient_data)`** patients 

Patients per clinic: **`r min(table(patient_data$clinic_id))`-`r max(table(patient_data$clinic_id))`**  

DV: **Pain Score** (0–100 scale)

</small>

]

.pull-right-50[

.center[
**Clinic-Level Predictors**

<small><small>

| Predictor | Type | 
|-----------|------|
| `r ji('hospital')` Clinic Size | Continuous | 
| `r ji('pill')` Protocol Type | Binary | 

</small></small>

]
]

---

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=T}

set.seed(46811)

plot_df <- patient_data[patient_data$clinic_id %in% 1:6, ]
plot_df$clinic_label <- paste0("Clinic ", plot_df$clinic_id)

# Seattle Children's color palette
sc_palette <- c(
  "#00B5AD",  # Teal
  "#8DC63F",  # Bright Green
  "#00A9E0",  # Light Blue
  "#EB7B35",  # Orange
  "#A7A9AC",  # Warm Gray
  "#4B4F54"   # Dark Gray
)
p8 <- ggplot(
  plot_df,
  aes(
    x = factor(clinic_label),
    y = pain_score,
    color = clinic_label
  )
) +

  geom_jitter(
    position = position_jitter(width = 0.15),
    alpha = 0.65,
    size = 4
  ) +

  stat_summary(
    fun = mean,
    geom = "crossbar",
    width = 0.5,
    color = "#4B4F54",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 20),
    limits = c(0, 100),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(values = sc_palette) +

  labs(
    title = "Pain Scores Across Six Clinics",
    subtitle = "Horizontal bars represent clinic means",
    x = "Clinic",
    y = "Pain Score"
  ) +

  theme_classic(base_size = 18) +

  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "#4B4F54"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

ggsave(here::here('slides','img','clinic_plot.png'), plot = p8, width = 6.5, height = 6.5, dpi = 300)

```

.center[

<img src=`r here::here('slides','img','clinic_plot.png')` style="width:550px; display:block; margin:-40px auto -20px auto;">

<small>

Patients in the **same clinic** may be more similar to each other than to patients in other clinics.

</small>

]

---

## `r ji('triangular_ruler')` Model Series Overview

```{r model-overview-table}

model_tbl <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Model 0", "Model 1", "Model 2", "Model 3"),
  Name  = c("Single-Level Null", "Empty MLM (Null)", 
            "Main Effects", "Interaction"),
  Predictors = c("None", "None (+ random intercept)",
                 "Clinic Size + Protocol Type",
                 "Size × Protocol Interaction"),
  Parameters = c(
    attr(logLik(Model0), "df"),
    attr(logLik(Model1), "df"),
    attr(logLik(Model2), "df"),
    attr(logLik(Model3), "df")
  ),
  AIC = round(c(AIC(Model0), AIC(Model1), AIC(Model2), AIC(Model3)), 1),
  BIC = round(c(BIC(Model0), BIC(Model1), BIC(Model2), BIC(Model3)), 1)
)

kable(model_tbl, align = "llllrr") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),
                full_width = TRUE, font_size = 18) |>
  row_spec(2, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f4fd") |>
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)

```

There are many more possible extensions. 

---

## <img src="`r here::here('slides','img','cfa_icon_emoji.png')`"style="width:60px; display:inline-block; margin-left:-10px; vertical-align:-12px;">   From 1-level to 2-level model

---

## <img src="`r here::here('slides','img','cfa_icon_emoji.png')`"style="width:60px; display:inline-block; margin-left:-10px; vertical-align:-12px;">   From 1-level to 2-level model

.pull-left-50[
.center[
.highlight[**<span style="color:#FFFFFF;">1-level Intercept Only</span>**]

$$y_i = \beta_0 + \text{e}_i$$

**ONE** source of variance:
$$\text{e}_{i} \sim N(0, \sigma_\text{e}^2)$$
<br>
<small>
*i* = level 1, <span style="color:#EB7B35;">*j*</span> = level 2
</small>
]
]
.pull-right-50[
.center[
.highlight[**<span style="color:#FFFFFF;">2-level Intercept Only</span>**]

$$\text{Level 1: } y_{i\color{#EB7B35}{j}} = \beta_{0\color{#EB7B35}{j}} + \text{e}_{i\color{#EB7B35}{j}}$$
$$\text{Level 2: } \beta_{0\color{#EB7B35}{j}} = \gamma_{00} + u_{0\color{#EB7B35}{j}}$$
**TWO** sources of variance:
$$\text{e}_{ij} \sim N(0, \sigma_\text{e}^2)$$
$$u_{0j} \sim N(0, \tau_{00})$$
]
]

---

## <img src="`r here::here('slides','img','cfa_icon_emoji.png')`"style="width:60px; display:inline-block; margin-left:-10px; vertical-align:-12px;">   From 1-level to 2-level model

.highlight[**<span style="color:#FFFFFF;">1-level Intercept Only</span>**]
<br>
.left[
**R code:**
<pre style="font-size:0.7em;">
m0 <- lm(y ~ 1, data = df)
summary(m0)
</pre>
] 

.highlight[**<span style="color:#FFFFFF;">2-level Intercept Only</span>**]
<br>
.left[
**R code:**
<pre style="font-size:0.7em;">
m1 <- lmer(y ~ 1 + (1 | cluster), data = df)
summary(m1)
</pre>
] 

---

## <img src="`r here::here('slides','img','cfa_icon_emoji.png')`"style="width:60px; display:inline-block; margin-left:-10px; vertical-align:-12px;">   Model 0 Single-Level Null

---

## <img src="`r here::here('slides','img','cfa_icon_emoji.png')`"style="width:60px; display:inline-block; margin-left:-10px; vertical-align:-12px;">   Model 0 Single-Level Null

.center[

$$
y_i = \color{#00B5AD}{\beta_0} + \color{#EB7B35}{\text{e}_i}
$$

**Fixed Effect**

<small>

| Parameter | Estimate | SE |
|-----------|----------|----|
| $\color{#00B5AD}{\beta_0}$ (Mean) | `r round(summary(Model0)$coefficients[1], 2)` | `r round(summary(Model0)$coefficients[2], 2)` |

</small>

**Random Effects**

<small>

| Source | Variance | SD |
|--------|----------|----|
| Total ($\color{#EB7B35}{\sigma_\text{e}^2}$) | `r round(summary(Model0)$sigma^2, 3)` | `r round(summary(Model0)$sigma, 3)` |

</small>

]

---

## <img src="`r here::here('slides','img','cfa_icon_emoji.png')`"style="width:60px; display:inline-block; margin-left:-10px; vertical-align:-12px;">   Empty MLM (Null)

---

## <img src="`r here::here('slides','img','cfa_icon_emoji.png')`"style="width:60px; display:inline-block; margin-left:-10px; vertical-align:-12px;">   Empty MLM (Null)

.center[

**Fixed Effect**

<small>

| Parameter | Estimate | SE |
|-----------|----------|----|
| $\gamma_{00}$ (Grand Mean) | `r round(fixef(Model1)[1], 2)` | `r round(sqrt(diag(vcov(Model1)))[1], 2)` |

</small>

**Random Effects**

<small>

| Source | Variance | SD |
|--------|----------|----|
| Between-Clinic ($\tau_{00}$) | `r round(tau_u0, 3)` | `r round(sqrt(tau_u0), 3)` |
| Within-Clinic ($\sigma_\text{e}^2$) | `r round(sigma_e, 3)` | `r round(sqrt(sigma_e), 3)` |
| **Total** | **`r round(tau_u0 + sigma_e, 3)`** | |

</small>

]

---

## `r ji('bar_chart')` Intraclass Correlation (ICC)

**What is the ICC?**

$$\text{ICC} = \frac{\tau_{00}}{\tau_{00} + \sigma_\text{e}^2} = \frac{`r round(tau_u0, 3)`}{`r round(tau_u0, 3)` + `r round(sigma_e, 3)`} = `r round(icc_manual, 3)`$$

**Three Interpretations**  

<small><small>

- **Proportion:** `r round(icc_manual * 100, 1)`% of variance in pain is **between clinics**

- **Correlation:** Patients from the same clinic correlate at **r = `r round(icc_manual, 3)`**

- **Effect Size:** `r ifelse(icc_manual < 0.05, "Small", ifelse(icc_manual < 0.10, "Medium", "Large"))` clustering effect

</small>

> `r ji('warning')` No ICC value is “safe” to ignore.

</small>

```{r icc-pie, fig.height=4.5, fig.width=4.5, eval=F}

pie_df <- data.frame(
  Source   = c("Between-Clinic\n(ICC)", "Within-Clinic"),
  Variance = c(tau_u0, sigma_e),
  Pct      = c(icc_manual, 1 - icc_manual)
)
ggplot(pie_df, aes(x = "", y = Variance, fill = Source)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, color = "white", linewidth = 1) +
  coord_polar("y") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Pct * 100, 1), "%")),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            size = 6, fontface = "bold", color = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#2196F3", "#FF7043")) +
  labs(#title = "Variance Partition",
       fill  = NULL) +
  theme_void(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        legend.position = "bottom")
```

---

## `r ji('zap')` Design Effect

**Clustering inflates variance (less info than you think)**

$$
\small
\text{DEFF} = 1 + (\bar{n} - 1) \times \text{ICC}
$$
$$
\small
= 1 + (`r round(avg_cluster_size, 1)` - 1) \times `r round(icc_manual, 3)` = `r round(design_effect, 2)`
$$

**`r ji('warning')` Effective Sample Size**

$$
\small
N_{\text{eff}} = \frac{N}{\text{DEFF}} = \frac{`r nrow(patient_data)`}{`r round(design_effect, 2)`} \approx `r round(effective_n, 0)`
$$

---

## `r ji('thinking')` Likelihood Ratio Test 

**Hypothesis:** 

$\small H_0: \tau_{00} = 0 \space \color{gray}{\text{(no clustering)}} \quad \quad H_1: \tau_{00} > 0 \space \color{gray}{\text{(clustering exists)}}$

**Result:** 

$\small -2 \Delta \text{LL} (df = `r attr(logLik(Model1), "df") - attr(logLik(Model0), "df")`) = `r round(as.numeric(lrt_stat), 2)` \text{, p } `r if(pchisq(as.numeric(lrt_stat), df = attr(logLik(Model1), "df") - attr(logLik(Model0), "df"), lower.tail = FALSE) < 0.001){"< .001"} else {paste0("= ", round(pchisq(as.numeric(lrt_stat), df = attr(logLik(Model1), "df") - attr(logLik(Model0), "df"), lower.tail = FALSE), 4))}`$

<style="clear: both;">

.center[
.highlight[
**<span style="color:#FFFFFF;">
Model improves!
</span>**
]
]

**R code:**
<pre style="font-size:0.7em;">
lrt_stat <- -2 * (logLik(Model0) - logLik(Model1))
p_value <- pchisq(lrt_stat, df = 1, lower.tail = FALSE)
</pre>

---

## `r ji('face_with_head_bandage')` Expected Pain Score Range

$$\small \text{95% CI} = \gamma_{00} \pm 1.96\sqrt{\tau_{00}} \quad = `r round(fixed_int, 2)` \pm 1.96 \times `r round(ri_sd, 2)` = [`r round(ri_lower, 1)`, \; `r round(ri_upper, 1)`]$$
```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=T}

x_seq <- seq(35, 65, length.out = 300)
dens  <- dnorm(x_seq, mean = fixed_int, sd = ri_sd)
shade <- data.frame(x = x_seq, y = dens)

p9 <- ggplot(shade, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_area(data = subset(shade, x >= ri_lower & x <= ri_upper),
            fill = "#2196F3", alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, color = "#1565C0") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = fixed_int, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "#E53935", linewidth = 1) +
  #annotate("text", x = fixed_int, y = max(dens) * 1.05,
  #         label = paste0("Grand Mean\n", round(fixed_int, 1)),
  #         hjust = 0.5, size = 3.5, color = "#E53935") +
  labs(#title = "Expected Distribution of Clinic Means",
       #subtitle = paste0("95% of clinics: ", round(ri_lower, 1),
       #                  " – ", round(ri_upper, 1)),
       x = "Mean Pain Score", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 36) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

ggsave(here::here('slides','img','pain_ci_plot.png'), plot = p9, width = 12, height = 6.5, dpi = 300)

```

.center[

<small>

**Expected Distribution of Clinic Means**

`r paste0("95% of clinics: ", round(ri_lower, 1), " – ", round(ri_upper, 1))`

</small>

<img src=`r here::here('slides','img','pain_ci_plot.png')` style="width:580px; display:block; margin:10px auto -20px auto;">

]

---
class: inverse, center, middle

## <img src="`r here::here('slides','img','cfa_icon_emoji.png')`"style="width:60px; display:inline-block; margin-left:-10px; vertical-align:-12px;">   Model 2: Main Effects

Adding Level-2 Predictors: 

Clinic Size & Protocol Type

---

## Model 2: Equations & Fixed Effects

.pull-left-50[

<small>

$$\color{grey}{\text{Level 1: }} y_{pc} = \beta_{0c} + \text{e}_{pc}$$

$$\small \begin{split} \color{grey}{\text{Level 2: }} \beta_{0c} = \gamma_{00} \\ + \gamma_{01}(\text{size50}_c) \\ + \gamma_{02}(\text{protocol}_c) \\ + U_{0c} \end{split}$$

**R code:**

</small>

<pre style="font-size:0.658em;">
Model2 <- lmer(
  pain_score ~ 1 + size50 + 
    protocol_type + (1 | clinic_id),
  data = patient_data, REML = TRUE)
</pre>


]

.pull-right[
**Fixed Effects**

<small><small>

```{r m2-fixed, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=T}

fe2 <- as.data.frame(coef_table2)
fe2 <- round(fe2, 3)
rownames(fe2) <- c("γ₀₀ Intercept", "γ₀₁ Size50", "γ₀₂ Protocol")
colnames(fe2) <- c("Estimate", "SE", "df", "t", "p")

kable(fe2[, c("Estimate","SE","t","p")], align = "rrrr") |>
  kable_styling(font_size = 15, full_width = TRUE) |>
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) |>
  column_spec(5, bold = TRUE,
              color = ifelse(fe2$p < .05, "green", "gray"))

```

**Intercept**: Expected mean pain = `r round(fixef(Model2)[1], 2)` for clinic size of 50 patients on the Standard protocol

**Size50**: `r round(fixef(Model2)[2], 2)` pts per each additional patient past 50

**Protocol**: Enhanced clinics `r abs(round(fixef(Model2)[3], 2))` pts `r ifelse(fixef(Model2)[3] < 0, "lower", "higher")` given clinic size of 50 patients.

</small></small>
]

---

## Model 2: Variance & Model Fit

.pull-right-45[

<small><small>

<style="clear: both;">

### $\text{Pseudo-}R^2$ (7.5% of the 28.3% possible)

<small></small>

```{r pseudo-r2-m2, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=T}

pr2 <- pseudo_r2_all[, c("Component","Base_Var","Model1_Var","PseudoR2_M1")]
colnames(pr2) <- c("Component", "Base Var", "Model 2 Var", "Pseudo-R²")
kable(pr2, align = "lrrr") |>
  kable_styling(font_size = 16, full_width = TRUE) |>
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)

```

</small><small>

### $\text{Total R}^2$ (`r round(totalR2_2 * 100, 2)`% of the total variance)

</small></small>

]

.pull-left-50[

**LRT: Model 1 vs Model 2**

<small><small>

```{r lrt-m1m2, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=T}

lrt12 <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Model 1 (Empty MLM)", "Model 2 (Main Effects)"),
  Params = c(3, 5),
  AIC   = round(c(AIC(Model1_ML), AIC(Model2_ML)), 2),
  Chisq = c(NA, round(lrt_compare$Chisq[2], 3)),
  df    = c(NA, lrt_compare$Df[2]),
  p     = c(NA, round(lrt_compare$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2], 4))
)
kable(lrt12, align = "lrrrr", na.string = "—") |>
  kable_styling(font_size = 15, full_width = TRUE) |>
  row_spec(2, bold = TRUE,
           background = ifelse(lrt_compare$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2] < .05,
                               "#e8f5e9", "#fff3e0"))

```

</small>

**Conditional ICC**
After adding Level-2 predictors:  

ICC = **`r round(cond_icc, 3)`** (`r round(cond_icc * 100, 1)`% between-clinic variance remains)

</small>

]

---
class: inverse, center, middle

## <img src="`r here::here('slides','img','cfa_icon_emoji.png')`"style="width:60px; display:inline-block; margin-left:-10px; vertical-align:-12px;">   Model 3: Interaction

Adding the Size x Protocol Interaction

---

## Model 3: Equations & Fixed Effects

.pull-left-50[

$$\small \begin{aligned} 
\color{grey}{\text{Level 2: }} \beta_{0c} = \, &\gamma_{00} \\ 
&+ \gamma_{01}(\text{size50}_{c}) \\ 
&+ \gamma_{02}(\text{protocol}_{c}) \\ 
&+ \gamma_{03}(\text{size50}_{c} \times \text{protocol}_{c}) \\ 
&+ U_{0c} 
\end{aligned}$$

<small>

**R code:**

<pre style="font-size:0.8em;">
Model3 <- lmer(pain_score ~ 1 
+ size50 * protocol_type 
+ (1 | clinic_id),
  data = patient_data, REML = FALSE
</pre>

<small><small>

| Protocol | Simple Slopes |
|----------|-----------|
| Standard | $\gamma_{01}$ = `r round(fixef(Model3)[2], 2)` |
| Enhanced | $\gamma_{01} + \gamma_{03}$ = `r round(fixef(Model3)[2] + fixef(Model3)[4], 2)` |

</small></small>

]

.pull-right-50[

**Fixed Effects**

```{r m3-fixed, echo=FALSE, fig.align="center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, eval=T}
fe3 <- as.data.frame(coef_table3)
fe3 <- round(fe3, 3)
rownames(fe3) <- c("γ₀₀ Intercept", "γ₀₁ Size50",
                   "γ₀₂ Protocol", "γ₀₃ Size×Protocol")
colnames(fe3) <- c("Estimate","SE","df","t","p")

kable(fe3[, c("Estimate","SE","t","p")], align = "rrrr") |>
  kable_styling(font_size = 14, full_width = TRUE) |>
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) |>
  row_spec(4, background = "#fff9c4") |>
  column_spec(5, bold = TRUE,
              color = ifelse(fe3$p < .05, "green", "gray"))
```

<small>

> Interaction $\gamma_{03}$ = `r round(fixef(Model3)[4], 2)`, *p* = `r round(coef_table3[4, 5], 3)`

</small>

]

---

## Interaction Visualization

```{r interaction-plot, fig.height=4.8, fig.width=10, fig.align='center'}

pred_data <- expand.grid(
  size50        = seq(-2, 2, by = 0.1),
  protocol_type = c(0, 1)
)
pred_data$clinic_size    <- pred_data$size50 * 50 + 150
pred_data$predicted_pain <- predict(Model3, newdata = pred_data, re.form = NA)
pred_data$Protocol       <- factor(pred_data$protocol_type,
                                   labels = c("Standard", "Enhanced"))

ggplot(pred_data, aes(x = clinic_size, y = predicted_pain,
                      color = Protocol, linetype = Protocol)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.8) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = predicted_pain - 1.5,
                  ymax = predicted_pain + 1.5,
                  fill  = Protocol), alpha = 0.1, color = NA) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Standard" = "#EB7B35", "Enhanced" = "#00B5AD")) +
  scale_fill_manual( values = c("Standard" = "#EB7B35", "Enhanced" = "#00B5AD")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(50, 250, 50)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Predicted Pain Scores by Clinic Size and Protocol Type",
    subtitle = "Model 3 — fixed-effects predictions (shading = +/- 1.5 pts illustration)",
    x        = "Clinic Size (number of patients)",
    y        = "Predicted Mean Pain Score",
    color    = "Protocol", linetype = "Protocol", fill = "Protocol"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

```

<small><small>

> The lines diverge as clinic size increases — this is the interaction. Enhanced protocol shows steeper improvement in larger clinics. Within our observed data range, the lines have not yet crossed, so there is no difference (*p* > .05).

</small></small>

---

## Interaction Visualization 

```{r interaction-plot2, fig.height=4.8, fig.width=10, fig.align='center'}

pred_data <- expand.grid(
  size50        = seq(-2, 5, by = 0.1),  # extended to reach clinic_size = 400
  protocol_type = c(0, 1)
)
pred_data$clinic_size    <- pred_data$size50 * 50 + 150
pred_data$predicted_pain <- predict(Model3, newdata = pred_data, re.form = NA)
pred_data$Protocol       <- factor(pred_data$protocol_type,
                                   labels = c("Standard", "Enhanced"))

ggplot(pred_data, aes(x = clinic_size, y = predicted_pain,
                      color = Protocol, linetype = Protocol)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.8) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = predicted_pain - 1.5,
                  ymax = predicted_pain + 1.5,
                  fill  = Protocol), alpha = 0.1, color = NA) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Standard" = "#EB7B35", "Enhanced" = "#00B5AD")) +
  scale_fill_manual( values = c("Standard" = "#EB7B35", "Enhanced" = "#00B5AD")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(50, 400, 50)) +  # extend breaks to 400
  labs(
    title    = "Predicted Pain Scores by Clinic Size and Protocol Type",
    subtitle = "Model 3 — fixed-effects predictions (shading = +/-1.5 pts illustration)",
    x        = "Clinic Size (number of patients)",
    y        = "Predicted Mean Pain Score",
    color    = "Protocol", linetype = "Protocol", fill = "Protocol"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

```

<small><small>

> Extending the x-axis reveals where the lines eventually cross - the point at which one protocol overtakes the other. This is where the Standard vs. Enhanced protocols are significantly different. 

</small></small>

---

## Model 3: Fit & Comparison

.pull-left-50[

**LRT: Model 2 vs Model 3**

```{r lrt-m2m3}
lrt23 <- data.frame(
  Model  = c("Model 2 (Main Effects)", "Model 3 (Interaction)"),
  Params = c(5, 6),
  AIC    = round(c(AIC(Model2_ML), AIC(Model3_ML)), 2),
  Chisq  = c(NA, round(lrt_compare23$Chisq[2], 3)),
  df     = c(NA, lrt_compare23$Df[2]),
  p      = c(NA, round(lrt_compare23$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2], 4))
)
kable(lrt23, align = "lrrrr", na.string = "—") |>
  kable_styling(font_size = 15, full_width = TRUE) |>
  row_spec(2, bold = TRUE,
           background = ifelse(lrt_compare23$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2] < .05,
                               "#e8f5e9", "#fff3e0"))
```

Model does not improve.

]

.pull-right-50[

.center[
**Pseudo-R2 Comparison**
]

```{r pseudo-r2-all-v2}

pr2b <- pseudo_r2_all[, c("Component","PseudoR2_M1","PseudoR2_M2","Change")]
colnames(pr2b) <- c("Component", "Model 2", "Model 3", "Δ var")
kable(pr2b, align = "lrrr") |>
  kable_styling(font_size = 15, full_width = TRUE) |>
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)

```

.center[
**Total R2**
]

<small><small><small>

| Model | Total R² |
|-------|----------|
| Model 2 (Main Effects) | `r round(totalR2_2 * 100, 2)`% |
| Model 3 (Interaction)  | `r round(totalR2_3 * 100, 2)`% |
| $$\Delta R^2$$ | **`r round((totalR2_3 - totalR2_2) * 100, 3)`** |

</small></small></small>

]

---

## `r emo::ji('rocket')` Next Steps & Extensions

**Immediate Next Steps**

<small>

`r emo::ji('bust_in_silhouette')` **Add Level-1 predictors** (patient age, diagnosis severity, visit frequency). This is where `r emo::ji('rocket')`

- `r emo::ji('chart_with_upwards_trend')` **Test random slopes** - do Level-1 effects vary by clinic?

- `r emo::ji('twisted_rightwards_arrows')` **Cross-level interactions** - do clinic characteristics moderate patient-level effects?

`r emo::ji('up')` **Consider three-level** structure (visits - patients - clinics)

</small>

---

class: inverse, center, middle

# `r emo::ji('mortar_board')` Thank You!   

Additional Questions?

.pull-left-45[

.left[

<small>

Key Takeaways:
`r emo::ji('one')` Always check for clustering (ICC) in clinical data
`r emo::ji('two')` ICC = `r round(icc_manual, 3)` which was huge (still MLM is *almost always* essential anyway)
`r emo::ji('three')` Effective N = `r round(effective_n, 0)`, not `r nrow(patient_data)` watch out for the design effect when thinking about powerign your study

</small>

]
]

.pull-right-45[

.left[

<small>

The MLM Advantage

`r emo::ji('white_check_mark')` Accounts for non-independence
`r emo::ji('white_check_mark')` Partitions variance appropriately
`r emo::ji('white_check_mark')` Handles unbalanced cluster sizes
`r emo::ji('white_check_mark')` Correct standard errors & inference

</small>

]

]
